user.title: Energy Sources
user.description: Step 1/2: Connect AURUM to your energy sensors. You need at least a grid meter sensor.

data_description.grid_power_entity: Your grid meter sensor (e.g. Shelly 3EM, SMA, Fronius, Kostal). Must report current power in watts. Positive = drawing from grid, negative = feeding into grid. This is the most important sensor — AURUM can't work without it.
data_description.pv_power_entity: How much power your solar panels are generating right now (in watts). Only used for display, not for calculations. Can be left empty.
data_description.battery_soc_entity: How full your home battery is right now (in percent, 0–100%). Leave empty if you don't have a battery — AURUM will send all surplus power directly to your devices.
data_description.battery_charge_power_entity: How much power is flowing INTO your battery right now (in watts, always positive). This is crucial for accurate surplus calculation — without it, AURUM can't see power that's 'hidden' in battery charging. Usually found in your inverter or BMS integration.
data_description.battery_discharge_power_entity: How much power is flowing OUT of your battery right now (in watts, always positive). Together with charge power, AURUM calculates the true PV surplus available for your devices.
data_description.pv_forecast_entity: Sensor for remaining PV energy today in kWh (e.g. Solcast 'Remaining today'). When set, activates the PV Budget module — AURUM will distribute surplus more intelligently based on the day's forecast.
data_description.pv_forecast_today_entity: Sensor with hourly forecast data as attribute (e.g. Solcast 'Forecast today' with forecast attribute). Enables precise sunset detection for budget calculation. Can be left empty.
data_description.pv_actual_today_entity: Daily counter sensor for PV energy produced today in kWh. AURUM uses this to compare forecast vs. reality and adjust the budget accordingly. Can be left empty.
data_description.weather_entity: Weather forecast entity (e.g. OpenWeatherMap, Met.no). AURUM uses weather conditions to improve forecast safety factor accuracy. Can be left empty.

battery.title: Battery Settings
battery.description: Step 2/2: How should AURUM handle your battery? If you don't have a battery, you can keep the defaults.

data_description.battery_capacity_wh: How large is your battery? Enter in watt-hours. Examples: 5,000 Wh = 5 kWh, 10,000 Wh = 10 kWh.
data_description.target_soc: At what charge level should all devices be allowed to run? Default: 80%. While the battery is below this, AURUM only runs devices with special permission.
data_description.min_soc: Below what charge level should ALL devices be turned off? Protects your battery from deep discharge. Default: 10%.
data_description.update_interval: How often AURUM checks sensors and controls devices (in seconds). 15 seconds works well for most setups. Usually doesn't need to be changed.

abort.already_configured: AURUM is already set up. You can configure it under Settings → Integrations.

data.action: What would you like to do?

settings.title: Energy & Battery
settings.description: Change your sensors and battery settings.

data_description.grid_power_entity: Your grid meter sensor in watts. Positive = drawing from grid, negative = feeding in.
data_description.pv_power_entity: Current solar production in watts (optional, display only).
data_description.battery_soc_entity: Current battery charge level in percent.
data_description.battery_charge_power_entity: Power flowing into battery (watts, always positive). Enables accurate surplus calculation.
data_description.battery_discharge_power_entity: Power flowing out of battery (watts, always positive).
data_description.pv_forecast_entity: Remaining PV energy today in kWh. Activates the Budget module when set.
data_description.pv_forecast_today_entity: Sensor with hourly forecast data as attribute. Enables precise sunset detection. Can be left empty.
data_description.pv_actual_today_entity: Daily PV production counter in kWh. AURUM compares forecast vs. reality and adjusts budget. Can be left empty.
data_description.weather_entity: Weather forecast entity for improved budget accuracy. Can be left empty.
data_description.battery_capacity_wh: Battery size in watt-hours (e.g. 10,000 Wh = 10 kWh).
data_description.target_soc: Above this level, all devices are allowed. Below, individual device thresholds apply.
data_description.min_soc: Below this level, ALL devices are turned off (battery protection).
data_description.update_interval: How often AURUM checks and controls, in seconds. Default: 15s.
data_description.notify_service: Receive push notifications on your phone when devices are turned on/off. Enter your notify service name, e.g. 'mobile_app_christian'. Find it under Developer Tools → Services → notify. Leave empty to disable.
data_description.price_entity: Current electricity price in ct/kWh. Works with Tibber, Nordpool, aWATTar, EPEX Spot or any price sensor. Enables price-aware device scheduling.
data_description.price_level_entity: Price level classification (very_cheap/cheap/normal/expensive/very_expensive). Used as fallback when no max_price is set on a device. Can be left empty.
data_description.cheap_period_entity: Binary sensor that is ON during cheap price periods (e.g. tibber_prices best price period). Devices with 'cheap grid' mode will run during these periods. Can be left empty.
data_description.cheap_period_starts_in_entity: Sensor showing minutes until the next cheap price period starts (e.g. tibber_prices 'Bestpreis startet in'). Used to display countdown in device status. Can be left empty.

add_device.title: Add Device
add_device.description: Add a device that AURUM should control with solar power. Any smart plug that shows up in Home Assistant works.

data_description.name: Give the device a unique name, e.g. 'Washing Machine', 'Pool Pump' or 'EV Charger'. This name will appear as a sensor in Home Assistant.
data_description.switch_entity: The smart plug that turns this device on and off. Just pick it from the list — all available plugs and switches are shown.
data_description.power_entity: Does your smart plug measure power consumption? Then select its power sensor here. AURUM will use the real consumption instead of the estimate. Can be left empty.
data_description.nominal_power: How much power does this device use when running? Usually written on the device label. Examples: Washing machine ~ 2,000W, Pool pump ~ 500W, EV charger ~ 3,700W. Used as estimate when no power sensor is set.
data_description.priority: Which devices should get solar power first? Higher number = more important. Recommendation: 80–100 for washing machine/dishwasher, 50–70 for pool pump/water heater, 20–40 for EV charger.
data_description.soc_threshold: When the battery drops below the charge target: at what battery level is this device still allowed to run? Set to 0 if the device should only run with full surplus.
data_description.startup_detection: Enable for appliances that run programs (washing machines, dishwashers, dryers). AURUM automatically detects when a program starts and protects it from being interrupted — even when the sun goes behind clouds.
data_description.sd_power_threshold: Power above which a program start is detected. Set higher than standby but lower than running load. Example: 50W for a washing machine. Default: 5W.
data_description.sd_standby_power: Power the device draws when plugged in but idle (Shelly self-consumption ~1–2W + device standby). Default: 3W.
data_description.sd_finish_power: Power below which the program is considered finished. Usually same as standby. Default: 3W.
data_description.sd_finish_time: How long power must stay below finish threshold before AURUM marks program as done. Default: 600s (10 min).
data_description.sd_detection_time: How long power must stay above start threshold before a program start is confirmed. Default: 5s.
data_description.sd_max_runtime: Safety limit: maximum allowed program runtime in seconds. AURUM marks program as done if exceeded. Default: 10800s (3h).
data_description.deadline: When does this device need to be finished? Example: 18:00 if laundry needs to be done by dinner time. AURUM will start the device in time — even without solar surplus. Leave empty if there's no time requirement.
data_description.estimated_runtime: How long does a typical cycle take? Examples: Washing machine ~ 120 min, Dishwasher ~ 150 min. Used together with the deadline so AURUM knows when to start.
data_description.interruptible: Can AURUM turn this device off when surplus drops? Disable for devices that must not be interrupted (e.g. 3D printer, aquarium heater). Default: on.
data_description.price_mode: Solar only: device runs exclusively on PV surplus — never uses grid power. Cheap grid: device primarily runs on PV surplus, but ALSO turns on when electricity is cheap (below your max price or during best-price periods) — even at night or on cloudy days. Both modes always prefer solar when available. Requires a price sensor in settings.
data_description.max_price: Maximum electricity price in ct/kWh at which this device may use grid power. Set to 0 to use price level / cheap period detection instead. Only applies in 'Cheap grid' mode.
data_description.residual_power: Extra safety margin added to the surplus requirement. Prevents the device from consuming power that's needed for grid stability. Default: 100W.
data_description.hysteresis_on: Extra surplus required beyond nominal power before turning on. Prevents flapping when surplus barely covers the device. Default: 200W.
data_description.hysteresis_off: How far surplus can drop below zero before turning off. Higher = more tolerant of short cloud cover. Default: 100W.
data_description.debounce_on: How long surplus must be sufficient before turning on (seconds). Prevents switching on during brief sun spikes. Default: 300s (5 min).
data_description.debounce_off: How long deficit must persist before turning off (seconds). Lets the device ride through short clouds. Default: 600s (10 min).
data_description.min_on_time: Once turned on, how long must the device stay on (seconds)? Protects devices that don't like short cycles. Default: 600s (10 min).
data_description.min_off_time: Once turned off, how long must the device stay off (seconds)? Useful for compressors, fans with cool-down. Default: 60s.

edit_device.title: Edit Device
edit_device.description: Change the settings for this device.

data_description.name: Give the device a unique name, e.g. 'Washing Machine', 'Pool Pump' or 'EV Charger'.
data_description.switch_entity: The smart plug that turns this device on and off.
data_description.power_entity: Power sensor of the smart plug (optional, for real consumption).
data_description.nominal_power: How much power does this device use when running?
data_description.priority: Higher number = more important. Gets solar power first.
data_description.soc_threshold: Below charge target: at what battery level is this device still allowed?
data_description.startup_detection: Enable for appliances with programs (washers, dishwashers). Protects running programs.
data_description.sd_power_threshold: Power above which a program start is detected. Example: 50W for a washing machine. Default: 5W.
data_description.sd_standby_power: Power the device draws when idle (Shelly + device standby). Default: 3W.
data_description.sd_finish_power: Power below which the program is considered finished. Default: 3W.
data_description.sd_finish_time: How long power must stay below finish threshold before marking done. Default: 600s.
data_description.sd_detection_time: How long power must stay above start threshold to confirm program start. Default: 5s.
data_description.sd_max_runtime: Safety limit for maximum program runtime in seconds. Default: 10800s (3h).
data_description.deadline: When does this device need to be finished? Leave empty if no time requirement.
data_description.estimated_runtime: How long does a typical cycle take in minutes?
data_description.interruptible: Can AURUM turn this off when surplus drops? Disable for must-not-interrupt devices.
data_description.residual_power: Extra safety margin added to surplus requirement (W).
data_description.hysteresis_on: Extra surplus beyond nominal power before turning on (W).
data_description.hysteresis_off: How far surplus can drop below zero before turning off (W).
data_description.debounce_on: How long surplus must be sufficient before turning on (seconds).
data_description.debounce_off: How long deficit must persist before turning off (seconds).
data_description.min_on_time: Minimum time the device stays on once started (seconds).
data_description.min_off_time: Minimum time the device stays off after being turned off (seconds).

remove_device.title: Remove Device
remove_device.description: Choose a device to remove from AURUM control.

options.price_mode_solar_only: Solar only – runs only on PV surplus (default)
options.price_mode_cheap_grid: Solar + cheap grid – also runs when electricity is cheap, even without solar
